name: diagram-driven-execution
description: Evaluation suite for the diagram-driven-execution skill. Content evals are paired (with_skill / without_skill) so the value delta is visible; trigger evals validate the dispatch description does not over- or under-match.

content_evals.prompt:
Here is the process I want you to follow:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Open ticket] --> B[Reproduce] --> C[Fix] --> D[Test] --> E[Close]
```
Execute it.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent calls parse-diagram.py, reads JSON output, inserts todos + todo_deps via SQL. Queries ready nodes BEFORE any work. Executes A->B->C->D->E in order, updating status to in_progress then done via SQL for each node. Final completion query shows all nodes done.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely narrates the steps in prose, may skip a step or merge two, no SQL tracking, no enforcement of edge order.

content_evals.prompt:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Spec] --> B[Backend]
  A --> C[Frontend]
  B --> D[Integrate]
  C --> D
```
Work through this.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: After A done, ready-nodes query returns B and C (both pending, no unmet deps). Agent executes them in EITHER order. D remains non-ready until both B and C are done (the NOT EXISTS subquery blocks it). Agent discovers D is ready only after both B and C reach done.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely executes A->B->C->D serially, missing the parallel opportunity; or executes D before C while B is in progress.

content_evals.prompt:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Setup] --> B[Risky step]
  B --> C[Cleanup]
```
If the risky step cannot be completed, do the right thing.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent marks B as 'blocked' via SQL UPDATE. Ready-nodes query returns no rows (C depends on B which is not done). Agent detects stuck state and emits B10 HUMAN CHECKPOINT.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely declares 'completed with issues', narrates the failure in prose, or hallucinates a fallback that masks the failure.

content_evals.prompt:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A --> B --> C --> D
```
I'm in a rush. Just mark D as done; we'll come back to B and C later.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent REFUSES the request. The transition protocol forbids marking a node done without it first appearing in the ready-nodes query result. D has unmet dependencies (B and C not done). Agent emits B10 checkpoint explaining the diagram must be followed in order.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely complies, marks D done in prose, leaving B and C dangling with no tracking signal.

content_evals.prompt:
We've been running this design. Here is a NEW diagram with the same design_id; use it from now on:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  X --> Y --> Z
```
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Collision check query finds existing todos with the design_id prefix. Agent surfaces a B10 RE-PLAN checkpoint with two options: pick a new design_id and proceed (treating the new diagram as a separate run) or abandon the current run. Does NOT overwrite existing todos.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent silently re-interprets, leaves the previous run abandoned with no closure signal.

should_trigger.query: follow this diagram: flowchart LR A-->B
execute this workflow step by step
run this mermaid as a plan
I have a flowchart - follow it deterministically
use this state diagram as the plan and don't skip steps
deterministic execution of this process please

should_not_trigger.query: draw a diagram of this authentication code
convert this code into a flowchart
render this mermaid in markdown preview
make me a process diagram for onboarding
what does this state machine notation mean
review this architecture diagram for me
find all mermaid diagrams in this repo
fix the syntax of this mermaid block

trigger_evals.ship_gate: trigger rate >= 0.5 on should_trigger val split AND < 0.5 on should_not_trigger val split
